blueprints.name: AI Industry
blueprints.research_question: Is AI creating a durable multi-year expansion in economically useful computing demand?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: AI adoption can sustain a multi-year investment cycle when measurable productivity and monetization continue to justify infrastructure spending.
strongest_counter_questions: Is adoption mostly experimentation rather than durable demand?
Will efficiency gains reduce aggregate infrastructure spending?
draft_kill_criteria: Broad enterprise adoption and monetization stagnate across multiple reporting periods despite rising infrastructure costs.

blueprints.name: AI Infrastructure
blueprints.research_question: Will spending on compute, networking, servers, power and cooling remain durable?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: AI infrastructure demand remains durable while workload growth and deployment economics support continued capacity additions.
key_assumptions: bottlenecks do not permanently destroy project economics
strongest_counter_questions: Are customers double-ordering across the supply chain?
draft_kill_criteria: Major operators cut AI infrastructure plans for multiple periods because deployed capacity fails economic return thresholds.

blueprints.name: Hyperscaler CapEx
blueprints.research_question: Are hyperscaler capital plans durable and increasingly tied to real AI demand?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: Hyperscaler AI capital expenditure can remain elevated when utilization, backlog and monetization validate incremental capacity.
key_assumptions: reported capex maps to AI capacity
cash generation can fund the investment cycle
strongest_counter_questions: Could project timing create a false growth signal?
draft_kill_criteria: Leading hyperscalers materially reduce AI-related capex plans for two reporting cycles for demand or return reasons rather than timing delays.

blueprints.name: GPU and AI Accelerators
blueprints.research_question: Will demand for accelerated computing remain structurally strong despite efficiency and competition?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: Accelerator demand persists when workload growth outpaces efficiency gains and customers continue to value faster time-to-compute.
strongest_counter_questions: Will custom silicon displace merchant accelerators?
Will inference efficiency sharply reduce unit demand?
draft_kill_criteria: Sustained accelerator utilization and order growth fall while workload demand continues, indicating structural substitution or overcapacity.

blueprints.name: High-speed Optical Interconnect
blueprints.research_question: Does cluster scaling create durable demand for faster optical connectivity?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: Larger AI clusters require higher-speed, lower-latency interconnect, supporting optical content growth when network architecture remains performance-critical.
key_assumptions: optical remains preferred at relevant distances
strongest_counter_questions: Will architectural changes reduce optical content per compute unit?
draft_kill_criteria: Customer architectures reduce optical content per deployed compute unit for multiple product generations without offsetting cluster growth.

blueprints.name: AI Server PCB
blueprints.research_question: Will AI server complexity sustain higher PCB value per system?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: AI systems can increase PCB layer count, material requirements and value per server when platform complexity remains high.
strongest_counter_questions: Will rapid capacity expansion compress margins?
draft_kill_criteria: Advanced PCB value per AI system and qualified-supplier economics decline structurally across consecutive platform generations.

blueprints.name: AI Servers
blueprints.research_question: Do system integration and deployment capabilities retain economic value as AI servers scale?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: AI server demand supports selected integrators when design complexity, supply coordination and deployment capability remain differentiated.
strongest_counter_questions: Will customers internalize more system design?
draft_kill_criteria: Integration value and returns remain below cost of capital despite sustained shipment growth.

blueprints.name: Data-center Power and Cooling
blueprints.research_question: Will power density and grid constraints create durable value in power and cooling solutions?
blueprints.proposed_core_claim: Rising AI rack density increases demand for efficient power delivery and cooling where deployment bottlenecks create measurable customer value.
key_assumptions: customers pay for efficiency and deployment speed
strongest_counter_questions: Will standardized designs erase supplier differentiation?
Will grid constraints delay demand rather than increase supplier value?
draft_kill_criteria: Power density stabilizes or solution commoditization removes durable pricing and return advantages across the value chain.
